description:
Replace the global capped fixpoint (MAX_FIXPOINT_ITERS=8 added by
fz-f6e) with bottom-up SCC processing. Removes the cap; bounds
termination structurally via per-SCC widening.

## Why

The cap is a band-aid. Without it, recursive list-walk fns (map_l,
every_l, ...) shrink the tail Descr each iter and spawn specs
forever. The cap stops discovery globally — for fact_5 it converges
cleanly within 8 iters, but for map_l it produces 6 useless specs
with progressively-nested `tuple | list | fn | map` decoration that
no caller will resolve to. Pure noise.

The fixpoint also re-walks every spec every iter. For a non-recursive
100-fn module, that's 100*8 = 800 walks instead of 100.

## ELI5

Today every fn is in one room, going "anyone need anything?" over
and over. After 8 rounds we give up. Each round recursive
list-walks add nested-shape variants nobody calls.

What we want: process fns in caller-first order.
- main calls fact(5). fact processes once with input keys
  {any-key, [5]}.
- fact's body recurses with [int]. local fixpoint inside fact:
  add [int], type, no new keys, done.
- For map_l where the tail shrinks each iter: after 3 rounds,
  widen the new key (lift literal sets and shrink lists to
  list(any)). Bounded.

Two structures glued together:
- Tarjan SCC: groups of mutually-recursive fns processed together.
- Topological order: SCCs that call other SCCs go first.

## Plan

Phase 1: Bootstrap. Same as today — register every fn's any-key
spec, type body under all-any input.

Phase 2: Build call graph. Nodes = FnIds present in module.
Edges:
  - direct Term::Call / Term::TailCall callee
  - continuation fn_id from Term::Call / Term::CallClosure /
    Term::Receive
  - Prim::MakeClosure(fn_id, ...) target
  - fn_constants-resolved Term::CallClosure / TailCallClosure
    target (read from any-key spec's ft.fn_constants)
Edges to skip: Term::Return / Term::Halt (dynamic dispatch via
closure ptr), unknown CallClosure (any-key fallback).

Phase 3: Tarjan SCC → reverse-topological list. Reverse for
caller-first.

Phase 4: Per-SCC fixpoint.
  pending: HashMap<FnId, HashSet<Vec<Descr>>> accumulated across
  SCCs. Each SCC starts with whatever previous SCCs put in
  pending[F] for F in this SCC, plus any-key for F.
  For each SCC in topo order:
    local_pending = pending entries for fns in this SCC.
    for iter in 0.. {
      grew = false;
      for (F, key) in drain(local_pending):
        if !specs.contains((F, key)):
          specs.insert((F, key), type_fn(F, m, key));
          grew = true;
        Walk this spec's body. For each callsite (callee, args):
          let new_key = args_descrs;
          if callee in this SCC:
            let candidate = if iter >= widen_at {
              new_key.map(|d| widen(d))
            } else { new_key };
            local_pending[callee].insert(candidate);
          else:
            pending[callee].insert(new_key);
        For each cont site (cont.fn_id, slot0, captures):
          analogous: same SCC vs cross-SCC routing.
      if !grew { break; }
    }
  widen_at = 3.

Phase 5: drop dead any-keys (existing logic, line 397+).

Phase 6: Remove MAX_FIXPOINT_ITERS cap.

## File touches

src/ir_typer.rs: ~150-200 lines.
  - New: build_call_graph, tarjan_scc helpers (~80 lines).
  - Refactor type_module to drive per-SCC fixpoint.
  - Existing helpers (type_fn, effective_return_descr, narrow_for_if,
    etc.) unchanged.

No public API change. ModuleTypes shape stays the same.

## Acceptance

1. cargo test --workspace passes (453 + 9 + 1 deferred, same as
   today's baseline). No new failures.

2. Spec count for map_higher_order_renders_doubled_list drops:
   today the test produces 6 noise map_l specs (each with
   progressively-nested tuple|list|fn|map unions). With widening,
   should be ≤ 2 narrow specs + any-key.

3. Spec count for fact_5: 3 fact specs ([any], [5], [int]).
   Today's count after fz-f6e is 4 (any + [5] + [int] +
   [int|float]). The [int|float] comes from any-key body's
   `n - 1 = int|float`. Under bottom-up, any-key is bootstrapped
   but doesn't seed downstream keys for callees inside fact's SCC
   (its body's walk produces [int|float] but registers it as a
   pending key for fact, which is in its own SCC — and goes
   through local widening, but [int|float] is already widened).
   Acceptable if any-key's pollution survives as long as it stays
   isolated.

4. Compile time: no regression on existing fixtures. Ideally
   faster for non-recursive code (linear instead of O(specs *
   outer_iters)).

5. Drop fz-f6e's `MAX_FIXPOINT_ITERS` constant + `iter_count`
   tracking.

## Risks

- fn_constants discovered post-bootstrap may miss SCC cycles. Safe
  over-approximation: edges discovered later get any-key spec, no
  incorrectness — just less narrow than possible. Mitigate by
  re-running call-graph build after a final pass, but defer to a
  follow-up if needed.
- Tarjan implementation correctness — standard algorithm, will be
  unit-tested.
- Widening may over-widen for cases where natural convergence is
  possible. Mitigate by widen_at=3 (give 3 iters of natural
  convergence first).

## Out of scope

- Cont-seam typing (fz-ul4.27.22.2) — unblocked, separate ticket.
- Typed halt-cont (fz-ul4.27.22.3) — unblocked, separate ticket.
- fn_constants post-bootstrap re-discovery (filed as follow-up).
title: fz-typer.scc-fixpoint: bottom-up SCC-based spec discovery
